title: nabalus fraseri var. integrifolius
intro: Nabalus fraseri var. integrifolius, also known as the Fraser's hawk's beard or Nabalus integrifolius, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. These compounds are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may provide health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as a tincture or infused as a tea in herbal medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
